message:
test(bookmarks): collapse three escape-search assertions into helper

Three assertions inside `bookmarks::tests::escape_special_chars` (or
the equivalent percent/underscore/backslash search test) were
hand-rolling the same shape: call `store.search(query)`, unwrap it,
check `len() == 1`, then assert
`results[0].source_path == expected`. The duplication made it easy to
miss when the assertion shape was wrong (e.g. checking only the count
without the path, or vice versa) and noisy enough that the actual
test intent — "this query matches exactly one row by path" — wasn't
obvious.

Extract `assert_single_search_path(store, query, expected_path)` to
the test module and replace each duplicated block with one call. The
helper:

- Runs the query, collects all returned `source_path` values into a
  `Vec<&str>` so the failure message names the actual paths matched
  rather than just a length mismatch.
- Asserts the vec is exactly `vec![expected_path]` so any mismatch
  in count, ordering, or path content fails identically.
- Includes the original `query` literal in the failure message via
  `{query:?}` so the harness reports which of the three escape
  classes regressed.

Pure test refactor — no production code changes and the assertions
covered are unchanged in scope.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
